description: Sample tasks for context-pack evaluation against the repomind repo. Tasks 001-006 target the memory subsystem (code_review_graph/memory/) which is the only classified module on this repo. Tasks 007-008 are deliberate stress tests that target files outside the classified module — expected coverage is 0%, showing a real product limitation.

tasks.description: Add a new CLI command to the memory subsystem
tasks.notes: commands.py is in the memory module; should surface it reliably

tasks.description: Improve confidence scoring in the memory classifier
tasks.notes: Both classifier.py and graph_bridge.py are in the memory module

tasks.description: Refactor the context builder to support token budget limits
tasks.notes: context_builder.py is in the memory module

tasks.description: Change how memory refresh planning decides which artifacts to regenerate
tasks.notes: refresh.py is in the memory module

tasks.description: Add a new MCP tool to expose freshness metadata from .agent-memory
tasks.notes: metadata.py is in the memory module; tools.py is at the root package level (not in the module)

tasks.description: Update the graph bridge to expose co-neighbor relationships for classification
tasks.notes: Both files are in the memory module

tasks.description: Fix a bug in the Tree-sitter parser for Python files
tasks.notes: STRESS TEST — parser.py is in the root code_review_graph/ package, which is not a classified module. Expected coverage: 0%. This demonstrates the product gap for flat repo layouts.

tasks.description: Generate a D3.js visualization of the dependency graph
tasks.notes: STRESS TEST — visualization.py is in the root package, not a classified module. Expected coverage: 0%. Shows the limit of directory-keyword classification.
